title: salvia japonica f. erythrophylla
intro: Salvia japonica f. erythrophylla, also known as Japanese red salvia, is a subspecies of the Salvia japonica plant, a flowering perennial native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, derived from its bioactive compounds such as salviol and ursolic acid. The health benefits of Salvia japonica f. erythrophylla include relief from colds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used in traditional Chinese medicine and other folk remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
